methods.description: Procedure for preparing ancient common millet samples and determining their stable carbon isotope composition (δ13C) using mass spectrometry.

steps.description: Stable δ 13 C composition analyses were carried out on all 67 serial and bulk common millet samples from the five sections, each composed of three to five grains, without lemma.
steps.searchable_summary: Select common millet samples (3-5 grains each, without lemma) for stable isotope analysis.
inputs: Ancient common millet samples from archaeological sections

steps.description: Each sample portion was placed in a beaker and covered with a 1 % hydrochloric acid solution to remove any carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform acid wash on samples using 1% hydrochloric acid to remove carbonates.

steps.description: The samples were then washed with deionized water to pH > 5
steps.searchable_summary: Wash acid-treated samples with deionized water until pH is greater than 5.

steps.description: and oven-dried at 40 • C for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Oven-dry washed samples at 40°C for 24 hours.

steps.description: The dried samples were ground in an agate mortar and homogenized,
steps.searchable_summary: Grind and homogenize dried samples using an agate mortar.

steps.description: then vacuumsealed in a quartz tube with copper oxide and silver foil
steps.searchable_summary: Vacuum-seal ground samples in a quartz tube with copper oxide and silver foil.
outputs: Sealed quartz tubes containing samples and reagents

steps.description: and combusted for at least 4 h at 850 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: Combust sealed samples at 850°C for at least 4 hours.

steps.description: The CO 2 gas from the combustion tube was extracted and cryogenically purified.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract and cryogenically purify CO2 gas from combustion products.

steps.description: The isotopic ratio of the extracted CO 2 gas was determined using a MAT-253 gas source mass spectrometer with a dualinlet system at the Institute of Earth Environment, Chinese Academy of Sciences.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the isotopic ratio (δ13C) of purified CO2 gas using a MAT-253 gas source mass spectrometer.

steps.description: All isotope ratios were expressed using the following δnotation: (1) The isotopic standard used was Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite (VPDB);
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate δ13C values using the standard delta notation formula relative to the VPDB standard.

steps.description: analytical precision at the 1σ level was reported as 0.2 ‰.
steps.searchable_summary: Report the analytical precision of the δ13C measurement.

methods.description: Procedure for selecting archaeological sites and sections, collecting sedimentary material, and extracting ancient common millet remains.

steps.description: All the ancient common millet remains used in this study were found at five archeological sites in the Guanzhong Basin, i.e., the Baijia, Huiduipo, Manan, Beiniu, and Nansha sites (State Cultural Relics Bureau, 1998).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and select five archaeological sites in the Guanzhong Basin for sampling.
inputs: List of archaeological sites in Guanzhong Basin
Site characteristics (location, culture, area, layer thickness)
outputs: Selected archaeological sites (Baijia, Huiduipo, Manan, Beiniu, Nansha)

steps.description: Five sections characterized by continuous and well-developed sedimentation were selected for sampling at the Baijia, Beiniu, Huiduipo, Manan, and Nansha sites.
steps.searchable_summary: Select five sections within the chosen sites characterized by continuous and well-developed sedimentation.

steps.description: Slice sampling was applied to continuously sampling and the interval was 10 cm for the Baijia and Nansha sections, and 20 cm for the Beiniu, Huiduipo, and Manan sections (Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform continuous slice sampling of selected sections at specified depth intervals (10 cm or 20 cm).
outputs: Sampled sedimentary material from defined intervals

steps.description: Forty-liter sample bags were filled with sufficient quantities of sedimentary material
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sampled sedimentary material in 40-liter sample bags.

steps.description: to screen through a 50-mesh sieve to obtain samples using flotation (Tsuyuzaki, 1994).
steps.searchable_summary: Process sedimentary material using flotation and a 50-mesh sieve to extract archaeological remains.

steps.description: Different archeological remains were separated in the laboratory after air-drying.
steps.searchable_summary: Air-dry extracted samples in the laboratory.

steps.description: Different archeological remains were separated in the laboratory after air-drying.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate different types of archaeological remains from the dried samples.

steps.description: Agricultural seeds were identified and picked out under the stereomicroscope,
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and pick out agricultural seeds, specifically common millet, using a stereomicroscope.
outputs: Identified and picked agricultural seeds (common millet)

steps.description: then marked in order according to sampling depth.
steps.searchable_summary: Organize and label selected seeds based on their original sampling depth.

steps.description: The number of remnant common millet samples derived from all five sections is listed in Table 1.
steps.searchable_summary: Count and report the number of common millet samples obtained from each section.
outputs: Count of samples per section (e.g., in a table)

methods.description: Analysis of historical precipitation data for the Guanzhong Basin to characterize the climate context of the archaeological sites.

steps.description: Precipitation data from the Guanzhong Basin for the period 1951-2011 were analyzed (Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain historical precipitation data for the Guanzhong Basin for the period 1951-2011.

steps.description: The results showed that the precipitation for mid-June to September was between 110 and 526 mm, with a mean of 305 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the mean precipitation for the mid-June to September period from the time series data.

steps.description: The 95 % confidence interval for this mean is between 279 and 332 mm, ruling out the extreme values of abnormal years.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the 95% confidence interval for the mean precipitation.
